questions.q: How many subsets does a set with 4 elements have?
questions.explanation: A set with n elements has 2ⁿ subsets, since each element is independently either in or out, giving 2⁴ = 16. The answer 24 is 4 factorial, which counts orderings (permutations) of the elements, not subsets.

questions.q: In how many ways can you choose 2 items from a set of 5 distinct items, where order does not matter?
questions.explanation: This is the combination C(5,2) = 5! / (2! · 3!) = 10. The answer 20 corresponds to the permutation P(5,2) = 20, which counts ordered selections and therefore double-counts each unordered pair.

questions.q: What is the negation of the statement 'All cats are black'?
options: At least one cat is not black
questions.explanation: The negation of a universal statement ('all') is an existential statement: 'there exists at least one cat that is not black.' The statement 'No cats are black' is far stronger than the negation, which only requires a single counterexample.

questions.q: In graph theory, what is the degree of a vertex?
options: The number of edges incident to it
The number of vertices in the graph
The length of the longest path from it
questions.explanation: The degree of a vertex is the count of edges touching it, indicating how many neighbors it connects to. The total number of vertices in the graph is a property of the whole graph, not of any individual vertex.

questions.q: What is the union of the sets {1, 2, 3} and {3, 4, 5}?
questions.explanation: The union contains every element appearing in either set, with no duplicates, giving {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}. The single-element answer {3} is the intersection (elements common to both sets), not the union.

questions.q: The logical statement 'P AND Q' is true in which case?
options: When at least one of P, Q is true
When both P and Q are true
When exactly one of P, Q is true
When both P and Q are false
questions.explanation: Logical conjunction (AND) is true only when both operands are true. The condition 'at least one is true' describes the OR (disjunction) operator, which has a weaker requirement than AND.

questions.q: A proof technique that assumes the opposite of a claim and derives a contradiction is called what?
questions.explanation: Proof by contradiction assumes the statement is false and shows this leads to a logical impossibility, forcing the statement to be true. Proof by induction is a different method that establishes a base case and an inductive step rather than seeking a contradiction.

questions.q: How many distinct ways can 4 people be arranged in a row?
questions.explanation: The number of arrangements (permutations) of 4 distinct people is 4! = 4 × 3 × 2 × 1 = 24. The answer 16 is 2⁴ or 4², neither of which counts orderings of distinct objects.

questions.q: By De Morgan's law, NOT (P AND Q) is logically equivalent to what?
questions.explanation: De Morgan's law states that the negation of a conjunction equals the disjunction of the negations: NOT (P AND Q) = (NOT P) OR (NOT Q). The option (NOT P) AND (NOT Q) is the negation of (P OR Q) instead, applying the law to the wrong connective.

questions.q: What is the cardinality of the empty set?
questions.explanation: The empty set contains no elements, so its cardinality (number of elements) is 0. The answer 1 confuses the empty set with the set containing the empty set, {∅}, which does have one element.
